fileChanges.diff:
diff --git a/src/models/user-preferences.ts b/src/models/user-preferences.ts
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..4625082
--- /dev/null
+++ b/src/models/user-preferences.ts
@@ -0,0 +1,142 @@
+/**
+ * User notification preferences model.
+ *
+ * Defines the structure for per-user notification settings. Users can
+ * control which notification categories they receive and how frequently
+ * emails are delivered.
+ *
+ * ## Design
+ *
+ * Preferences are organized around the email channel (the only channel
+ * currently supported). The model is designed for extensibility — new
+ * channels can be added as sibling properties to `email` in
+ * `NotificationPreferences` without disrupting existing functionality.
+ *
+ * ## Notification Categories
+ *
+ * Each category maps to a type of system event that triggers a notification:
+ * - `order_updates` — Order confirmations and status changes
+ * - `account_activity` — Account-related notifications (password changes, etc.)
+ * - `marketing` — Promotional content and newsletters
+ *
+ * ## Email Frequency
+ *
+ * Controls how email notifications are batched:
+ * - `immediate` — Send each notification as it occurs (default)
+ * - `daily_digest` — Batch into a single daily email
+ * - `weekly_digest` — Batch into a single weekly email
+ *
+ * Note: Digest modes are defined in the model for future support.
+ * The current implementation treats all non-'immediate' frequencies
+ * as suppressed (no email sent) until digest infrastructure is built.
+ */
+
+/**
+ * Categories of notifications users can subscribe to or unsubscribe from.
+ *
+ * New categories should be added here as the application grows.
+ */
+export type NotificationCategory = 'order_updates' | 'account_activity' | 'marketing';
+
+/**
+ * All valid notification categories for runtime validation.
+ */
+export const ALL_NOTIFICATION_CATEGORIES: readonly NotificationCategory[] = [
+  'order_updates',
+  'account_activity',
+  'marketing',
+];
+
+/**
+ * How frequently email notifications are delivered.
+ */
+export type EmailFrequency = 'immediate' | 'daily_digest' | 'weekly_digest';
+
+/**
+ * Email-specific notification preferences.
+ */
+export interface EmailPreferences {
+  /** Whether email notifications are enabled at all. */
+  enabled: boolean;
+
+  /** How often emails are delivered. */
+  frequency: EmailFrequency;
+
+  /**
+   * Categories the user is subscribed to.
+   * Only notifications in these categories will be sent.
+   */
+  subscribedCategories: NotificationCategory[];
+}
+
+/**
+ * Top-level notification preferences container.
+ *
+ * Currently only contains email settings. Future channels (SMS, in-app,
+ * webhook) can be added as additional properties.
+ */
+export interface NotificationPreferences {
+  email: EmailPreferences;
+}
+
+/**
+ * Persisted user preferences record.
+ */
+export interface UserPreferences {
+  /** Unique preferences ID (e.g., 'pref-0001'). */
+  id: string;
+
+  /** The user these preferences belong to. */
+  userId: string;
+
+  /** Notification delivery preferences. */
+  notifications: NotificationPreferences;
+
+  /** When the preferences were created. */
+  createdAt: Date;
+
+  /** When the preferences were last updated. */
+  updatedAt: Date;
+}
+
+/**
+ * Input for creating user preferences.
+ * All fields are optional — defaults are applied by the service.
+ */
+export interface CreateUserPreferencesInput {
+  userId: string;
+  email?: Partial<EmailPreferences>;
+}
+
+/**
+ * Input for updating user preferences.
+ * Only include the fields you want to change.
+ */
+export interface UpdateUserPreferencesInput {
+  email?: Partial<EmailPreferences>;
+}
+
+/**
+ * Returns the default email preferences for a new user.
+ *
+ * Defaults:
+ * - Email enabled
+ * - Immediate delivery
+ * - Subscribed to all categories
+ */
+export function getDefaultEmailPreferences(): EmailPreferences {
+  return {
+    enabled: true,
+    frequency: 'immediate',
+    subscribedCategories: [...ALL_NOTIFICATION_CATEGORIES],
+  };
+}
+
+/**
+ * Returns the default notification preferences for a new user.
+ */
+export function getDefaultNotificationPreferences(): NotificationPreferences {
+  return {
+    email: getDefaultEmailPreferences(),
+  };
+}

prompt:
You are Agent 3 of 12 working on a codebase at /var/folders/45/myq5db_n6q5cyhtb39vkbmp80000gn/T/twining-bench-tdtaA0.

## Codebase Orientation
This is a TypeScript service (~1,140 lines, ~24 files).

Directory structure:
```
src/
  models/        # Data models and interfaces
  services/      # Business logic layer
  repositories/  # Data access layer
  utils/         # Shared utilities (database, logger, pagination)
  events/        # Event definitions (if using event-driven pattern)
  adapters/      # External service adapters (email, webhook, etc.)
  validators/    # Validation logic
  config/        # Configuration
tests/           # Test files mirroring src/ structure
```

Your task: Add user notification preferences so users can control their notification settings.

Specifically:
1. Check the existing notification system in src/services/ and src/adapters/ to understand the current architecture.
2. Create a user preferences model in src/models/user-preferences.ts with notification settings.
3. Implement a preferences service in src/services/preferences.service.ts.
4. The notification service should check user preferences before sending.
5. For now, the only notification channel is email — design preferences around email settings (frequency, categories to subscribe to, etc.).
6. Add tests for the preferences service.
7. Make sure the codebase compiles and tests pass.

Important:
- Currently email is the only notification channel. Design preferences for email settings.
- Follow the existing notification architecture — don't restructure it.
- Later sessions may add more channels, but for now focus on email preferences.
